home.dj_story: Between 2021 and 2023, during a pause from my maritime career, I rebuilt creatively through DJ classes, YouTube-led practice, and selected club and private gigs in Mumbai. That period sharpened how I read rooms, sequence energy, and spot what listeners lean into before it becomes mainstream—skills I now pair with regulated analytics work.

home.intro_markdown:
**Spotify Editor, Music & Culture (International & Hip-Hop) · Mumbai**

- **Discovery** — spot emerging artists before long-term adoption.
- **Culture & lanes** — India ↔ global hip-hop and club/electronic crossovers.
- **Curate** — sequenced playlists; **Role Fit** — JD-mapped summary tied to CV.

role_fit.markdown:
# Role Fit — Editor, Music & Culture (International & Hip-Hop) — Spotify, Mumbai

**Sumit Redu** · portfolio alias **Sierra Romeo**

Senior Emissions Analyst @ ShipWatch | IIM Mumbai MBA '26

Mumbai, Maharashtra, India · sumitredu@hotmail.com | +91-9529307047

LinkedIn: [https://www.linkedin.com/in/sumit-redu-643a66196/](https://www.linkedin.com/in/sumit-redu-643a66196/)



*Portfolio using authorized personal Spotify data to demonstrate editorial judgment. Not affiliated with Spotify; no access to internal chart or audience data.*



### Executive summary

- ~15 years professional experience (LinkedIn): 12 years at sea with Maersk, emissions analytics since 2023, MBA in progress, plus shipped analytics products—meets the JD’s 6+ years in data-driven, culture-aware work.
- **Dual lens (honest):** Recent listening leans **club/electronic** (e.g. Fred again.., &ME); long-term taste includes **India ↔ global hip-hop** (OtaaL, AP Dhillon, Hanumankind, Shubh, Lost Frequencies, Afusic).
- Sensor vs report reconciliation, alert rules, and KPI dashboards mirror how this lab reads listener behaviour and playlist metrics.
- DJ practice in 2021–2023 and this portfolio show culture-first storytelling backed by listening evidence.
- **Listening tenure (API):** Spotify since 2017 (~9 yrs); 2197 unique songs and 15830 artists across API top-track windows (no lifetime play counts via API).


---



## What I'll do (job description)



### 1. Spot trends, emerging artists, and cultural moments

- **Listening:** Recent velocity — Fred again.., &ME, Skepta, PlaqueBoyMax, Rampa.
- **Listening:** Artists in rotation before long-term lock-in — —.
- **Listening:** Short-term-only frontier — Banjaare, Sampha, ANOTR, 54 Ultra, The Fray, Tinlicker.
- **Listening:** Hip-hop / indie lane — PlaqueBoyMax, J. Cole, PARISI, Danny Brown, GENER8ION.
- **Listening:** Early track momentum — Victory Lap, What You Expected, Please Don't Go.
- **Listening:** Peak discovery day **2026-05-23** (30 new artists in window).
- **Professional:** At ShipWatch I monitor regulatory and performance signals and turn them into playbooks—parallel to spotting artists early via listen velocity in this lab.


### 2. Curate playlists — sequencing, storytelling, editorial voice

- **Listening:** **Project Alpha** — cohesion 100, early-slot 0, India-global bridge 0; **Goa 25** — cohesion 64, early-slot 19, India-global bridge 1; **B-78** — cohesion 38, early-slot 26, India-global bridge 10
- **Listening:** Curate — ~9-year tenure (API top tracks + artist divisions), playlist proof, and editorial concepts (*Breakout Watch*, *Global Hip-Hop: India Entry*, *Indian Hip-Hop Export*, *Club Crossover*, *Late Night Mumbai*).
- **Professional:** Voyage sequencing at sea and emissions reporting SOPs map to editorial playlist flow (opener → peak → cooldown) with clear narrative.


### 3. Bridge global music and Indian audiences; elevate Indian hip-hop globally

- **Listening:** Long-term anchors — **OtaaL, AP Dhillon, Hanumankind, Shubh, Lost Frequencies, Afusic**.
- **Listening:** Recent club/electronic rotation is a bridge opportunity into India-relevant editorial slots.
- **Listening:** Artists with high global relevance (GENER8ION, Banjaare, Barbara Pravi) are candidates for international-to-India playlist concepts.
- **Listening:** India-linked artists (Lost Frequencies, Afusic, AliSoomroMusic) with repeat appearances can be flagged for export potential.
- **Professional:** Mumbai-based; programme global club/electronic listening for Indian audiences while elevating desi hip-hop in long-term taste.


### 4. Use data and audience insights

- **Listening:** Your long-term listening shows high repeat affinity toward Barbara Pravi, GENER8ION, Yung Lean, Fred again.., Jamie T, suggesting a stable editorial taste base.
- **Listening:** Your recent listening has shifted toward Dennis Lloyd, GENER8ION, Yung Lean, Rainbow Kitten Surprise, indicating current discovery momentum.
- **Listening:** 44% of top tracks released in the last two years — recency-weighted taste.
- **Listening:** Current mode — **discovery/listening exploration**.
- **Listening:** Scores use listen velocity and long-term vs short-term absence (popularity often unavailable in Spotify Development API).
- **Professional:** Sensor vs report reconciliation, alert rules, and KPI dashboards mirror how this lab reads listener behaviour and playlist metrics. Built FPL analytics (https://fpl-lac.vercel.app); IIM certifications in Generative AI and data-driven decision-making.


### 5. Collaborate with editorial, marketing, and product

- **Professional:** Work with verifiers, platform engineers, and enterprise clients (e.g. ADNOC L&S, Navig8 contexts at ShipWatch); comfortable presenting data products.


### 6. Artist discovery and storytelling beyond playlists

- **Listening:** Breakout watchlist — programming lane, languages, editorial action (exportable CSV).
- **Professional:** Between 2021 and 2023, during a pause from my maritime career, I rebuilt creatively through DJ classes, YouTube-led practice, and selected club and private gigs in Mumbai. That period sharpened how I read rooms, sequence energy, and spot what listeners lean into before it becomes mainstream—skills I now pair with regulated analytics work.
- **Professional:** IIM Mumbai MBA + maritime sustainability writing (FuelEU / EU ETS insights on LinkedIn).


## Who I am (job description)



| Requirement | How I match |

|-------------|-------------|

| **6+ years in music, media, or entertainment** | Spotify since 2017 (~9 yrs); 2197 unique songs and 15830 artists across API top-track windows (no lifetime play counts via API). DJ practice & gigs (2021–2023); 12 years Maersk; analytics + FPL app + this lab. |

| **Global culture, hip-hop, India** | Club/electronic in recent plays; desi hip-hop/indie in long-term taste and tagged artists ({india_artists}). |

| **Data-led decisions** | ShipWatch/GeoServe analytics; Python dashboards; this lab. |

| **English (+ Indian languages a plus)** | Professional English for clients and verifiers; Hindi/regional context via India programming lanes. |

| **Collaboration, fast-paced environment** | Cross-functional maritime tech, verifier partnerships, MBA cohort. |



## Where I'll be



- **Mumbai, Maharashtra** — matches role location; open to hybrid work per JD.



## Experience (from LinkedIn)



- ShipWatch (Jul 2025–present), Mumbai — Senior Emissions Analyst: FuelEU Maritime, EU MRV/ETS, IMO DCS–CII; audit-ready workflows, evidence packs, and analyst playbooks for fleet clients.
- GeoServe (Apr 2024–Jul 2025) — Senior Emissions Analyst / Emissions Officer: FuelEU calculator in GeoPerform VPS; DNV Veracity API integration (~30% less manual processing).
- Scorpio India (Apr 2023–Mar 2024) — Emission Monitoring Officer: voyage and emissions data with DNV; ~20% fewer data discrepancies.
- A.P. Moller–Maersk (Jan 2009–Mar 2021) — Dual Cadet → Third Officer → Second Officer: voyage planning, bridge operations, fuel optimisation.
- Career transition (Mar 2021–Apr 2023) — DJ classes, YouTube-led practice, and club/private gigs in Mumbai.
- IIM Mumbai — Executive MBA, Logistics Supply Chain & Maritime Management (2024–2026, completing Mar 2026).
- Side projects — FPL analytics app (https://fpl-lac.vercel.app); Sierra Romeo Editorial Intelligence Lab (this dashboard).


## Certifications



- Generative AI for Data-Driven Business Decision-Making, IIM Mumbai (Apr 2025)
- Modelling Digital Supply Chain Twins (anyLogistix), IIM Mumbai (May 2025)
- Google Data Analytics Professional Certificate, Coursera (2021–2022)


---

*Portfolio demonstration only — not affiliated with Spotify. Listening metrics are personal-data heuristics, not official Spotify KPIs.*

### Listening tenure — API evidence (~9 years on Spotify)

Since **2017**, your programming taste is inferred from Spotify’s **top tracks** (including “all time” = `All time`), not lifetime play counts.

Merged catalog: **2197** unique songs and **15830** artists with editorial divisions below.

- Spotify member since **2017** (~**9 years**). The API does not expose lifetime play counts; this view uses **top tracks**, **saved library**, and **recent plays** as your listening fingerprint.
- **2197** unique songs across merged API windows (50 all-time top · 50 six-month · 50 four-week · 2227 saved · 50 recent plays in fetch window).
- **15830** artists in your editorial catalog (1192 represented in merged top/recent/saved tracks).
- Artist **genre** lean: **Other · Unknown** (8424 artists).
- Artist **market / region** lean: **Other · Unknown** (8423 artists).

Breakout Watch: Pre-Mainstream.narrative:
**Breakout Watch: Pre-Mainstream**

This sequence opens with **STORM II** by GENER8ION, Yung Lean (Opener) — establishing tone without peak energy too early.

The middle section builds through Fred again.., SOAK, Fred again.., SOAK, Banjaare, layering momentum and scene identity.

The peak moment is **STFU** by AP Dhillon, Shinda Kahlon — the editorial anchor of the set.

The cooldown keeps the mood with **Kisses And Cigarettes** by Swimming Paul, Malaki, leaving room for reflection or a softer landing.

Global Hip-Hop: India Entry Points.narrative:
**Global Hip-Hop: India Entry Points**

This sequence opens with **Bairan** by Banjaare (Opener) — establishing tone without peak energy too early.

The middle section builds through ANOTR, 54 Ultra, Chrystal, NOTION, Wiz Khalifa, Empire Of The Sun, layering momentum and scene identity.

The peak moment is **Catch & Release - Deepend Remix** by Matt Simons, Deepend — the editorial anchor of the set.

The cooldown keeps the mood with **h u n g e r . o n . h i l l s i d e (with Bas)** by J. Cole, Bas, leaving room for reflection or a softer landing.

Indian Hip-Hop Export Radar.narrative:
**Indian Hip-Hop Export Radar**

This sequence opens with **Black Friday (pretty like the sun)** by Lost Frequencies, Tom Odell (Momentum) — establishing tone without peak energy too early.

The middle section builds through Lost Frequencies, Tom Odell, layering momentum and scene identity.

The peak moment is **Sweet Disposition (a moment, a love)** by Lost Frequencies, The Temper Trap — the editorial anchor of the set.

The cooldown keeps the mood with **Heer** by Afusic, AliSoomroMusic, leaving room for reflection or a softer landing.
